svg.text: Cubic Heterogeneities Superimposed on a Layered Medium

g.text: inside the cell — exact to O(ka)⁷.
Every point in the medium belongs to exactly one cube. The T₀ captures all physics inside each cell;
the G₀ captures all physics between cells. Together, they give the complete wavefield.
The G₀ propagator is the bottleneck: in a layered medium it requires the Kennett reflectivity recursion
(wavenumber integration, reflection/transmission matrices at each interface, mode conversions) for every
pair of cube centres. Horizontal translational invariance allows 2D FFT acceleration.
